# LITLE Trust Fabric — Institutional Architecture & Protocol Spec

> **"The Standard for Preserving Knowledge. Verifying Legacy."**
> LITLE is an institutional trust infrastructure designed for independent research, identity verification, and multi-generational knowledge preservation. Governed by the **7-federations framework of TAMV**, LITLE decouples the standard from the implementation, providing cryptographic lineage, deterministic identifiers, and Merkle-verifiable evidence chains.

---

## I. Architectural Pillars

### 1. LITLE-ID (RFC-0001)

A durable, self-describing, and namespace-aware identifier standard designed to outlive specific storage backends or binary formats.

* **Structure:** Supports both URI forms (`litle://2026/tech/IA/8F4A29D...`) and human-readable tokens (`LTL-2026-RQ-8F4A-29D3`).
* **Work Types:** Categorized cleanly across domains (e.g., `BK` for Books, `RQ` for Research, `DS` for Datasets, `PL` for Pipelines, `AR` for Articles).
* **Decoupled Cryptography:** Operates independently of underlying signature profiles (`L-512.v1`), allowing long-term asset identity to persist even as cryptographic primitives evolve or migrate.

### 2. Evidence Chain & Lineage DAG (RFC-0008)

A tamper-evident, Merkle-verifiable record of the entire research pipeline and provenance lifecycle.

* **Node Types:** Captures structured cryptographic and metadata nodes (`Source`, `Version`, `Author`, `Signature`, `PipelineStep`, `ModelUsage`, `Prompt`, `Seed`, `Checksum`, `Config`).
* **Verifiable Continuity:** Each node stores an immutable SHA-256 hash of its canonical payload, linking parents to children to ensure zero-trust auditability across automated and manual research workflows.

### 3. Governance & Standards Layer (RFC-0010)

An explicit standards framework separating protocol definition from application code.

* **7-Federations Model (TAMV):** Decentralized institutional oversight managed across seven autonomous operational and validation federations.
* **LIP Process:** LITLE Improvement Proposals governing the lifecycle of core specifications from Draft to Stable and Experimental states.
* **Independent Archive (RFC-0009):** Protocol commitments for multi-repository synchronization and long-term offline reproducibility.

---

## II. Cryptographic Engine & Verification

* **Profile L-512 v1:** Post-quantum-ready signature and container format providing robust integrity guarantees for preserved manuscripts and artifacts.
* **Zero-Trust Verification:** Publicly accessible validation channels enabling independent third-party verification of identity, lineage integrity, and institutional seals without requiring credentials.

---

## III. Platform Surface Map (Routes & Access Directory)

* **[Home / Landing Page](https://www.google.com/search?q=/)**: System overview, core pillar navigation, and infrastructure introduction.
* **[Public Verification (`/verify.$litleId`)](https://www.google.com/search?q=/verify.%24litleId)**: Real-time cryptographic status, metadata, and Evidence Chain summary lookup for any valid LITLE-ID.
* **[Standards Council & Governance (`/standard`)](https://www.google.com/search?q=/standard)**: Overview of the 7-federations governance model, the LIP lifecycle, and core vs. experimental specifications.
* **[RFC Index (`/standard/rfcs`)](https://www.google.com/search?q=/standard/rfcs)**: Complete registry of Request For Comments (RFC-0001 through RFC-0010) with active status indicators (Draft, Stable, Experimental).
* **[Independent Archive (`/standard/archive`)](https://www.google.com/search?q=/standard/archive)**: Replication guarantees, mirrored storage architecture, and decentralization roadmaps.
* **[Authenticated Archive Workspace (`/_authenticated`)](https://www.google.com/search?q=/dashboard)**: Restricted environment for pipeline ingestion, manuscript synthesis, and evidence node recording.
* **[Authentication Portal (`/auth`)](https://www.google.com/search?q=/auth)**: Secure identity gate for archive administrators and authorized contributors.
